summary: `mention-reachable` reads `mention_edges` alone, not the resolved edge set every other graph judge shares — a mention absent from that partial set gets zero coverage while its clause runs green

edit.description: The wiring gap. `graph::mention_reachable` (call site 1114) receives `&mention_edges` alone, while `graph::degree` (1108) receives `&edges` *and* `&mention_edges` — so a member whose mention rides a field edge resolved into `edges` but not the separate `mentions` family gets zero reachability coverage and the clause reports nothing (invariant-6 fail-open; human-verified at the consumer: three uncovered `consult` mentions, no findings, yet the same edges count for `degree`). `edges_from_declarations` (2428) and `mention_edges_from_declarations` (2468) build the two families independently; route the judge to the one resolved enumeration contract.md mandates — the READ-EDGE-UNIFY shape (6e80405: the read family consumes the gate's resolved set, no private re-derivation).

`mention_reachable` (343) filters `mention_edges` by `edge.from`; give it the unified resolved edge set every judge shares and select the rendering-claim (mention) property off it, so a mention carried on any field edge is judged rather than silently dropped. `degree` (236) is the precedent — it already takes both edge sets.

tests.asserts: a member whose mention rides a field edge is judged by `mention-reachable` (an out-of-scope target fires a finding) rather than silently passed because the mention was absent from a mention-only edge set

acceptance: `mention-reachable` ranges over the one resolved edge enumeration the gate and every read verb share (contract.md, 'edge': every edge resolves into one enumeration that the gate and every read verb share) — a mention carried on a field edge is judged, with no fail-open silence. `cargo test` green.
notes: Field defect (centercode; session-verified on disk, window d359782..26c0528 clean of src/). Both halves reproduced: three uncovered `consult` mentions get no finding, the same edges count for `degree`. Fix derivable, no new intent — the READ-EDGE-UNIFY bar (6e80405). Mention rows are emit-resolved, but the gap is read-side (main.rs wiring), so confirm before assuming an SDK ripple. scoped at 26c0528.

summary: A corpus `admit` over a host wipes its declared file-template layer: `templatesFor` returns admit rows instead of `facts.templates`, so composing a body over `skill` kills its `supporting-doc` layer

edit.description: `templatesFor` (193): when any embedded kind is admitted over the host it returns `admitted.map((kind) => ({ kind }))` (195) *instead of* `facts.templates`, and admit rows carry no `path` column — so a file layer (a template with a path pattern) is unspellable in the replacement, and the engine then refuses via `drift`'s nested-file-path check ('its host kind `skill` templates no file layer'). Admission overrides the embedded (pathless) grain only; join the path-carrying declared templates rather than replacing them. The doc comment (181-192) calls the override 'layer-blind' — that is the bug, not a licensed simplification; rewrite it to the joined semantics.

tests.asserts: admitting an embedded kind over a host that declares a file-template layer keeps that file layer (its child kind still templates a path), so a composed body over `skill` still projects `supporting-doc` files rather than being refused for no file layer

acceptance: An `admit` over a host overrides only the embedded (pathless) child kind and joins the host's path-carrying file-template layers — composing a body over `skill` still projects its `supporting-doc` files, and the nested-file-path refusal no longer fires on a host that declares a file layer (representation.md, 'nesting': admission is a declaration over the host kind; the template's path pattern is the host's declared fact). `pnpm --dir sdk test` + `cargo test` green.
notes: Field defect (centercode, migrating onto 0025; session-verified, consumer reverted to per-skill `at`-locus doc kinds). Fix derivable, no new intent: admission overrides a layer's child kind and admits the embedded grain — neither sanctions wiping a file layer. Change is SDK-side (`templatesFor`); TemplateRow's wire already carries an optional path, so `src/read.rs` is likely unchanged — confirm the `builtin_lock_frozen` seam. scoped at 26c0528.

summary: emit's embedded edge-leaf resolution does a flat `members.get(address)` over a `kind:name` table, so a bare address on a one-element `to` set (`['skill']`) dies 'resolves to no composed member'

edit.description: `edgeTargetFacts` (242): `options.members?.get(address)` (253) looks a bare leaf address up in a `${kind}:${name}`-keyed table (`memberTable`, 625), so a leaf like `harness-meta` on an edge field whose `to` is the one-element set `['skill']` never matches and throws 'resolves to no composed member' (255) — but `EdgeField.to`'s own doc (kind.ts:55, decision 0029) promises 'a one-element set resolves a bare address within its one kind'. Qualify a bare address to `${edge.to[0]}:${address}` when `edge.to.length === 1` before the get; a multi-element set still demands the written kind-qualified address.

tests.asserts: an embedded edge field whose `to` is a one-element set resolves a bare leaf address within that one kind (the derived edge-target facts point at the right member), matching what a kind-qualified address resolves to; a multi-element set still requires the qualified form

acceptance: An embedded member's edge field with a one-element `to` set resolves a bare address within its one kind (contract.md, 'edge': a one-element set resolves a bare name within its one kind; a multi-element set requires the kind-qualified address always) — emit derives the edge-target facts instead of refusing. `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Field defect (centercode, session-verified on disk). SDK-only: the resolution gap is in `edgeTargetFacts`; decision 0029 ratified bare-for-singleton, so the lookup fixes toward the doc `EdgeField.to` already carries. No Rust ripple — this is emit's own leaf resolution over the composed member table. scoped at 26c0528.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (59) sits under a doc (55-58) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (621) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (648), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 519 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 618-620 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 643-646 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..b85df4a — re-verified this tick — so every address carries.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1349) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1350-1354) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1352) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. The file is untouched across 0c3cbcb..b85df4a — re-verified this tick — so the addresses carry.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at b85df4a and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics. The window touched neither file, so every address carries. The constant is still 5 on disk (`graph.rs`:59, re-read this tick), so the park's subject is untouched — and may still be wrong. A build-side friction capture records the same disagreement; it is the human's to read, not plan's to drain. rescoped 4a33911; park re-tested b85df4a.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at b85df4a, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 8fc5e21..b85df4a -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.
